reviews.review_text:
top 5 reasons to play deep rock galactic:

Maps always feel different. 
You can drink beer.
You can pass out from drinking too much beer. 
You can play flappy bird. 
You can find jet boots in some games. Yes jet boots. Dwarf will fly. 


Honorable mention: the OST SLAPS

also, rock and stone!

I didn't fully understand this game when I first picked it up around its early access release. It sat forgotten in my library for a good year or two after that, gathering dust and honestly, I didn't even think about it. 

When I decided one day to try it out again I was shocked at why I didn't stick with the game the first time around. DRG has been like crack cocaine for me — the gameplay loop, weapons and gear customisation, enemy and mission variety — it has SO much content and SO much going on. 

The best thing by far about this game is its community though. I remember starting as a greenbeard and finding lobbies with veterans who patiently guided me and assisted me for hours on end. Now, after 900 hours I find myself returning the favour to newer players. It doesn't feel like a task or a chore, it brings genuine joy to help the community of a game that has helped me. 

Deep Rock Galactic is likely my favourite game of all time and I highly recommend this to anyone looking for a chill and friendly community and often chaotic missions full of adrenaline and pure fun.

So much better then the Dumpster Fire that is Helldivers 2

(Edit if helldivers 2 gameplay Improves then I can play that game again, but for now Rocking and Stoning it is)

(edit 2 Actually Helldivers 2 is not that bad, guess I was listening to Doomer's to much)

Deep Rock Galactic has to be one of the best co-op games ever made, here is what I liked:

Positives:
- Memorable characters.
- Goofy vibes.
- Rewarding cooperation and progression.
- Lots of content.
- Fun movement and combat.
- One of, if not the best implementation of a season pass.
- Rock and stone!

There are no significant negatives about the game, so the negatives section will be skipped.

I give it a score of 5/5.

I genuinely love Deep Rock Galactic. I’ve put hundreds of hours into it, bought cosmetics, convinced friends to play it, and defended the game for years because it used to feel like one of the few co-op games made by developers who actually cared.

But now it honestly feels abandoned.

Instead of properly supporting the game that built the community, the studio seems more interested in chasing trends with spin-offs like Survivor — which is basically “Vampire Survivors but with dwarves” — and now Rogue Core, which feels like another attempt to turn DRG into a roguelite instead of improving the original game people actually fell in love with.

Meanwhile the base game barely gets meaningful updates anymore. Not even regular balance tweaks, quality-of-life passes, weapon reworks, bugfixes, or hotfixes at the pace you’d expect from a live-service co-op game this popular. There are bugs and issues that sit around forever while development focus clearly goes elsewhere.

And before people spam “the game already has enough content” — that’s not the point. The point is that the original DRG became successful because of the gameplay loop, teamwork, and constant support. Now it feels like that support has slowed to a crawl while the brand gets stretched into side projects.

I’m not even saying Rogue Core or Survivor are terrible games. I’m saying it sucks watching the original game slowly lose momentum while the developers move on to copy-and-paste trend chasing.

DRG deserved better long-term support than this.

Rock and Stone… I guess.
DRG is great, community is great, devs are great, 9.8/10. the other .2 go to cave leeches.

Rock and stone!

It is a really good game with great community. 100% recommend it

This is a game that you can have a lot of fun on by both playing with friends [b]or[/b] by yourself. There's lots of stuff to do and the progression feels good. [Might feel a little slow at first. But once you get a hang of it, it will feel good.] The learning curve is good, however things might feel a little overwhelming at first. But you'll get up to speed pretty quickly!

I can personally play on Level 3 difficulty with no issues, Level 4 can be pretty challenging playing Solo. Depending on what mission type it is. [There are 5 levels of difficulties. 1-5]
You can also do lots of fun stuff in the mission control, by playing small mini games and drinking beer until you pass out! And the game it self is also pretty goofy!

[h3]Each mission feels different[/h3]
You have quite a few different mission [b]types[/b] and each mission has a secondary goal that will give you some extra bonus points! While some missions could also contain some hidden loot. So keep your eyes peeled!

[h3]In conclusion:[/h3]
I'm having lots of fun playing this game. It's a game that I can kick back, relax, shut my brain off, while keep my focus sharp. And it's not like it's the end of the world if I fail a mission either. So it's a good balance of keeping you focused in the game, but also do stuff without much thinking.

[b]Rock and Stone eveyone![/b]

For those who are on the edge about buying this, its a incredible experience. the gameplay is top notch and the atmosphere and visuals in this are grounding. I've spent moments just walking around admiring the space rig and its small details.

After 350+ hours i can confidently say this game has rocks and stones! and one more thing 
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.
.

"MuShRoOm"

[h1]Me and my friends been loving the latest "well crafted" new events the new team have been making on the clearly intended "difficulty" that the devs made![/h1]

Okay I have been holding this review in work for almost three years. 
But every time I opened this page, I feel like I keep getting distracted by new hats, or paints that I kept telling myself I will use. I'm not sure what that says about this game in particular, this ramble is what this game deserves. Rerrr! 

[h1]I would highly recommend this game if you do have 3 spare dwagons near you that you can dognap.[/h1]
[h2]Desc[/h2]
Deep Rock is a very simple game where some company sends you, 4 dwarfs to HOXXES IV to mine and shoot the same bugs. Again. and Again. And then get distracted by some shinies.
(...)
(Also also, if you really like playing alone instead. You can still do that and maybe PET the lootbugs along the way. They are STUPIDLY ROUND and make silly noises. Maybe you can also ask your assistant, Bosco that would at least would NEVER complain about it to help you .. hold Alt-Fire to mine gray rocks. They can also help by staring at you while some ceiling Cave Leech nibbles your muzzle and stuff, owo. So,, Its all worth it. I prolly need to play Solo Haz5+ to amplify the incredibly fun experience. The increased cave complexity +3, infinite insect swarm, and warnings sounds super fun! I love Bosco with its silly "..." voicelines. Mayb its a little shy.

[h2]THINGIES[/h2]
Some do say that the procedural map generation is not exactly exciting. But I do like the consistency of seeing Molley (your personal MULE) gaining the ability to float really makes sense. It keeps the game exciting and full of surprises. I like how many gray rocks there are in HOXXES too, I helped to distract me from noticing the amount of time I got lost in the caves. I would also like to mention how you can bring Doretta head's home with you. What a feature certainly worth mentioning! Now that is the best addition yet since the past whar? 2020?"

While the class and role variety is flexible enough in multiplayer, I think we have to praise how GS has been keeping up with player's need of enemy types. They really truly listened to us for the past few years!

We have bugs that you can headpat and do stuff?
One pulls you and nuke your health through walls, one blinds you. And then, and then, one explodes your whole team I think. I don't remember actually because I'm too busy falling into the same pre-generated holes. The point is, there are too much bugs! My friends kept telling me to watch out for Rockpox and Rival Nemesis or let alone these new Corespawn bugs, or something like that. but then I ask which one is that, before I immediately die anyway.
I believe if they would reinvent the wheel of actually adding new interactable enemy types or actual counterplay to literally anything, me and my friends three collective braincells would prolly die from having to think about them, especially on higher Haz+ and that is not why I clearly play this game for this many hours. Wearh!

By the time I do finish the mission, all I can think about are just grey and brown rocks. I genuinely don't remember anything from the incredibly creative mission types. We have 150 morkite mining, more aquarq mining, and, waiting for asteroid crackers, and OH! more mining but with pipes. I don't know what is there to complain about really. Each mission feels really new to me, thanks to the variety of 4? 6? 7? SIX SEVEN? 9? 10? mission types or something like that my .. paws can't even count that high.  I dunno, I kept forgetting what I did every time a new drop pod lands. I like how Deep Rock respects my time by making everything felt samey so that I don't really have to learn anything new, ever!

I think that the point - Deep rock is onto something by making each mission type felt like a JOB that keeps you around without paying you.

[h2]FEATURES[/h2]
[h3]And then, hear this .. You can press V at any time to yell "Rock and Stone!"[/h3] 
Most importantly, this precedes anything you do. Before a mission. During a Mission? Found Gold? After the host kicks you. Maybe. It is truly a revolutionary turn off your brain to interact with others button.

Yes, I know there is a strong competitor of pinging Gold where your dwarf will say "WE'RE RICH! we're RICH!" But, y'know .. If you went to the Fungus Bogs, there are these giant mushrooms. BUT BUT BUT! When you spam your [X] ping button, your dwarf will repeatedly say "MUSHROOM! MUSHROOM?! MUSHROOM." Once, I spent a silly amount of time doing so, while my allies were doing their own thing. I could certainly say the host was certainly not happy about it, similar whenever I tried to solo objectives. Apparently the guides and rule book (idk what they are talking about honestly) does not allow that.

The community is SUPER helpful too with how swift and efficient they are, thanks to the lack of voting system. There will be no drama or anything, they will type "..." and you will be gone.

Both the discord and Community sounds like a great place you will like, if that's what you are into! The feeling of losing all progress you did from that almost 1 hour game is unmatched, similar to getting kicked on last wave in MvM; I think it does feel like character building though. It really prepares you for some random Deep Dive crashes where you don't get anything, and maybe some day you will be as good as them.

[h2]QOL Pass & Late Game[/h2]
I think Ghost Ship did something right by adding free battle pass that lasts indefinitely instead of more biomes or new content. I'm not sure where they got they idea from, but hey more mil-sim and rainbow gun skins!! I know that whenever someone asks me about it, they would prolly say 'that's hideous, why would they add that?' and then I never really see them ever playing again. I don't know if that is related or was not related. I think I confused myself too.

Why not, besides well umm .. what is Nexus Mods anyway? Never heard of them, really. They kept telling me using sandbox modification in Singleplayer is illegal or something. I think I can trust them, since its their game after all.

Although, I'm not super sure what the community meant by the end-game content is lacking. I do think the weapon system is neat with how you need to wait 20-50 weeks or about 50-100 hours for the overclock you want. There are like hundreds of them, which are just sidegrades from one another. And you know what? 

They are not wrong, But, BUT! - I do like having a reason to have to log back in every week for a chance for 1+3 chance to roll a weekly core for a weapon or class I would never use. That's a respectful REALISM to have; It feels like a job! But LISTEN, when I do finally get what I wanted after another 100 hours, the ADHD in my brain somehow gave me a DOPAMINE to play another 50 hours or so. Something like that, yus. More please!

Not to mention how you can promote your dwarf to get a different colored border. Bronze. Silver. Gold. Platinum? 
[h3]Whoa no way. My brain goes "!!!" every single time.[/h3]
It’s a new color to look at. I like the colors. Its still something new to look at. I mean the gray rocks and music do feel nice, but COLORS. The promotion badges genuinely made me cry. I didn't know I needed that. I don't know why I kept playing. My campus' therapist told me that they should have named my son after GSG, and not Relogic after my Mediumcore Zenith seed experience.

[h2]Remarks[/h2]
I cannot express how else - or heck where I would be without this game!

I'm so happy to be put in this situation while getting my ass hammered on Hazard ~5+ thanks to its very realistic and friendly game design. I sure love getting bombed from literally everywhere thanks to its new corespawn bug design!! It really keeps me aware of my surroundings. Other games should really maybe teach PvE players to not fall asleep more.
P.S sorr my cat told me to stop.

Gold and Scales! uwu
Funny innovative coop game which also works surprisingly well solo.

Friend and I spent an hour or so building a super-long, super-fun track to get the Roller Coaster achievement.  Took so long...  We found there are limits to the map area!  :P  Swore not to do this again.

Mutual friend joins the chat an hour later, "Oh hey, I wanna do that!"  

So, we're gonna build another roller coaster!  LOL

11/10 perfect game.

Except for the Lithophage Corruptor; that thing can get stuffed with a pineapple.

If you're looking for a review and not a niche critique you should probably keep scrolling, though I'll say the game is satisfyingly punchy, has great character and various zones and weapons to explore. If you like Left 4 Dead and are wanting a little novelty, chances are you'll probably like this game too. I personally like this game, but it falls completely flat in the implementation of its reward structures. 

Typically when selecting a mission to complete, you'll choose a mission you have an assignment for. On successful completion of these assignment missions, you are awarded various currencies and gold to spend on shop goodies. Unfortunately, the implementation of missions and rewards feels at odds with the way the game is intended to be played in a couple prominent ways; multiplayer friction and difficulty vs play incentives.

For multiplayer, the issue is extremely straight forward: each player in a lobby is going to have a different mission for their assignment; meaning you cannot progress multiple players' missions at the same time. This obviously sucks for playing with friends, and will likely result in players rotating whose missions are completed each round. 

The part that baffles me is that the vast majority of missions have absolutely no reason to be asynchronous, given they're just random filler to get you exploring and exterminating Hoxxes. There's no story associated with most missions, so having them be exclusionary feels like a slap in the face to multiplayer incentives. You're going to complete your missions far quicker in solo lobbies; forcing people to choose between either progressing their missions at an even remotely reasonable pace alone, or playing with friends and slowing reward gain drastically.

My other issue with the game relates to the difficulty options for missions, and how that clashes with the rewards handed out on mission completions. In Left 4 Dead, the missions you run and difficulties you choose do not have any greater bearing on the game beyond moment-to-moment play. There are no in-game rewards or currencies to receive; difficulty is encouraged solely through the appeal of mastery.

Unfortunately, a similar implementation of difficulty in Deep Rock Galactic is at odds with the reward structure. Given that assignment rewards are only obtained from successful missions, players are incentivised to play on lower difficulties to breeze through their missions to guarantee their mission rewards. Forcing players to choose a fun and challenging gameplay experience or a boring but highly efficient one sucks.

I hope in the future that some of these systems undergo some renovations. At the very least, you should be able to (for weekly core and resource missions) sync your assignment with other players in the lobby.
nice game for relax or dynamic style based on complexity level selected

Beat Bosco's high score in jetty boot, best day of my life

The rest of the game is pretty good

So much fun with a group of friends. Deep Rock Galactic has a lot going on for it now, which can be overwhelming if you are just starting off, but once you find the right place to begin, the gameplay really clicks. This is a solid game.

Great game for killing time and bugs either playing solo, with friends, or with randoms.

Great atmosphere when spelunking (in levels), good weapon variety and build potential, hub area that encourages lingering and exploration with good environment design and seasonal decoration. Depending on what you like it might not be worth the tho I'd happily pay that if I knew what was being offered.

What a great game. It's like Helldivers except you're underground. CRAZY amount of content and things to do. Recommended to play with friends, but the community is super active so you will never need to play alone. 10/10
this is a perfect game to just divert yourself after a long day of hardwork , just dive in with your friends , complete missions and get to the drop pod. Rock and Stone!

One of the best PvE experiences I've ever had an honor to experience. Definitely on par with Left 4 Dead for me.

-Gameplay. Simply amazing. The map is basically your playground. Dig, build, kill bugs, have fun, as long as you get the job done, anything goes. Classes are amazing, they actually feel distinct, have absolutely different sets of weapons(and several weapons to choose for each class, and each of those weapons also has a distinct feeling), so it's always interesting to level up a different class. Gunplay is fine, it does its job and that's OK.

-Quests. They also cooked well here. Most of the quests are interesting and have an unique catch to them. For expect, build pumps and ride on them like on the roller coaster, collect crystals and bring them to the base(It has turrets!!!), destroy a big ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ corporate machine, defent the big drill, just collect some ♥♥♥♥ and get the ♥♥♥♥ outta there.  Also, the most important feature of the game is its procedural generation, so each level's layout is unique, has its biome(which are usually pretty cool!), some landmarks(like optional boss). You won't get bored for a very long time here, believe me.

-Story. Well, you are miners, you dig ♥♥♥♥, there are some infection, yeah. It doesn't have a story and it doesn't need it, it isn't a story game. But characters have a personality and it's interesting to listen to them. Each class has it's own phrases and character, the dispatcher is a pretty cool guy and has that "dad" feeling to him. They did well here.

-OST. It's pretty amazing and unique. It's synth, if you love that genre, you will this soundtrack. To some of the composition I still listen from time to time, they are that good.

-Graphics. It's a low-poly graphics, and it's done really well. It has its style and just is nice to look at.

-Technical side. No problems here, everything runs smoothly, zero bugs.

A must-play for any PvE fan, basic a fundamental game at this point. 10/10, buy for the full price and support the developer.

Probably the best CO-OP game that will ever exist. Devs keep winning.

ROCK and STONE!

11/10 - A must-play if you're a fan of FPS and Metroid looking for a variety filled challenge.
-------------------------
I've been playing on Xbox since 2022 and logged roughly 2k hours since, but only recently did I choose to start from scratch on Steam for fun. The game not only runs buttery smooth even with a few mods, but DRG just soars with everything it meant to: Amazing action and gameplay, rock-solid frame rate, amazing community, and whole catalogues of mods to try with built-in parts of the menu and startup just for those mods. 
My favorite parts of DRG are the cosmetics and progression. You can customize everything about your dwarves from head to toe, armor, armor paint, and tons of facial hair to name a few. But the best part is no matter what you unlock or how you unlock it, the game always makes you feel good about it like you earned it.
------------------
FOR ROCK AND STONE!!
(And go play Rogue Core if you want something even harder than DRG)

Лучший симулятор шахтёра с друзьями и даже с рандомами, ибо это самое братское сообщество которое я знаю.

10 подорванных на C4 Скаутов из 10

FOR ROCK AND STONE, BROTHER!

Did someone say rock and stone?? Because if they did, this is a very fun game, especially if you're into PvE like me. A lot of charm has been put into this and it's well worth the money. It's often on sale too!

ngl the game is more fun than I expected, even solo play is enjoyable
sucks you can't nuke the entire map with a press of a button though, would be pretty balanced imo

This game is Helldivers 2, just better and more fun in every way. Enough said. 

No pay to win. DLCs are cosmetics only. Buy the base game and you have access to everything through normal progression. And there is A LOT of progression. And it never stops being fun.

games good but lost 84 hours of data
tried all suggested methods of restoring lost save and it does not work, not sure why this even happens
i am understandably upset about this 👍🏻

It's so dark it's unplayable. Even if you play scout, your flares are limited and it's a chore maintaining them. Aside from that, the enemy movement isn't readable or fun so the aiming feels bad, and it's set underground in caves so you get lost a LOT. The game just isn't very fun unless you get a good team going, and even then it's mid. 

The players are super nice, though.

I'm still learning what everything does as the game has a lot of things to learn.

The controls are responsive, sound effects are good, weapons handle well, lots of character customisation, different builds for each character, different weapon combo's, good mission variation with various enemies that can attack while mining an area or returning to the mule.

They will also attack when returning to the pod once a mission is complete.

After each successful mission your character earns XP to level up, which will unlock other missions or areas to explore.

It's quite an addictive game.  Get in, mine what you can and get out, hopefully, in one piece. :D

I'm enjoying what I've played so far, but I have a lot more to learn.
Awesome game, I keep coming back to it again and again, Solo is doable and fun, but of course, go for it with your friends and its all nighter after all nighter ! (don't play on sundays)
Great game and great devs, always supporting the game and the community and years later its still a lot of fun to come back and play

This game is great with friends, it gets boring by yourself. Drink, Mine, and abuse Gun privliges. Yes this game is Awesome. Worth every penny. 

Dev to Dev review: Great play cycle, could use some work on play difficulty/progression. But great work Devs!

Warning: No I am not a dev of this game, although I do develop games.

Great replayability and very fun to play with or without friends. Exploring the caves and doing your objectives is a sort of puzzle solving excercise, and the swarms get very intense. The game has really great pacing as well, between the slow exploration phase and the fast swarm phase.
One of the best games I have ever played, which keeps winning more and more good will thanks to how much love it gives to players.
coffee stain is Swedish so kind of not good but other than that pretty solid fun game
Not a bad game, but, as with many games, gets repetitive if you play it long enough.
I absolutely adore this game it is so fun and the community and the funny little rituals are the best part 10/10 =D

Played this game a lot when I was on PlayStation. Got the game on PC, and even though I didn't get to keep my progress, I still had fun grinding though the missions and getting back to where I was. I like the game for it's personality and even it's design. The geometric shapes of everything fit the cave aspect pretty nicely. There's fun events and holidays. And every season is completely free as you can switch through them freely and grind any cosmetics you want or finish a season you didn't have the time for.
This games slaps like the dice after a mission. Best work culture ever. Always pints after a diggy diggy session.

Can be cozy at times, when you just goof around, mine, kill a few bugs or kill swarms. Or it can turn into a full on COD zombies experience, not just on high difficulties, me and my friend had one of the most eventful hazard 3 missions of all time, it was hell. I would recommend this game to anyone, 10 rocks out of 10 stones.

This is easily one of the best co-op games I’ve played. The whole thing just understands what makes playing with other people fun: everyone has a job, every class feels useful, and even a messy mission can turn into a great story if the team somehow makes it back alive.

The caves are a huge part of the magic. They’re dark, weird, dangerous, and different every time, so even simple objectives feel fresh. Mining, fighting bugs, calling supplies, digging tunnels, saving teammates, rushing back to the drop pod with no ammo left — it all comes together in a way that feels chaotic but never pointless.

I also love how much personality the game has. The dwarves, the voice lines, the beer, the hub area, the upgrades, the “Rock and Stone” energy — it makes the game feel warm and funny without ruining the tension of the missions.

It’s the kind of game that works whether you want a chill run with friends or a complete disaster where everyone is yelling and somehow still laughing. Very easy recommendation. Rock and Stone.

Almost certainly the best money I've spent in gaming. 
Fantastic coop experience when played with friends.

Deep Rock Galactic is easily one of the best games I’ve ever played. The gameplay loop never gets old, the classes all feel useful, the missions are chaotic in the best way, and the co-op is some of the most fun you can have with friends. It’s funny, intense, rewarding, and packed with personality from top to bottom.

10/10 — Rock and Stone forever.

This is a 10/10 game I'd recommend to anyone, any day.
Don't even hesitate, Just buy it, try it, and refund it if you don't like it. I dare you!
Rock and stone!
If you like this kind of game, you'll LOVE this game. It plays so well and its so fun even to mess around. Also I don't think any of my friends have failed to run this game smoothly.
Great game overall. Lots to do, high replay ability, and the player base is so wholesome and nice. Great for playing with friends because you get to see chaos ensue

Good game but MAKE OMEGA SHUT UP
HIS VOICE IS LIKE FORK ON METAL!!!!!

Bring Back Mission control or a Dwarf Commander Please

With over 200H on the PS, i can safely say that this game is a banger.
It's such a damn shame that there is no cross progression because starting from 0 does feel a bit unmotivating but i love this game so i can forgive it.
The classes are all pretty balanced and viable.
I do wish that we could maybe see new classes in the future and see some new content that changes the game in a strong and good way.
After 200H the game can start to feel a bit repetitive (obviously) which is why i think a new class could spice things up a bit.
By the way this game has 0 MICROTRANSACTION that are pay to win.
Only cosmetic items, that is a MASSIVE plus from me.
ROCK AND STONE TO THE BONE!

Deep Rock Galactic has been one of my most played and favorite games for a long time now. Where to even start! All the characters are fun to play, scout being my favorite. Driller being a close second. Grappling hooks are really fun, the flare gun is a very great tool and I adore the Deepcore with AI stability engine. This game has so much content variety and essentially endless build potential and things to do. The easy modding support is amazing especially given some of the extremely nice QOL stuff there is. My only gripe with DRG is the weapon overclock grind, which has been amended a bit by adding them to promotions from season 3 and onwards. But theres still around 150 or so of them and only like a third are transformative and id say fewer are actually class defining. Neuro Toxin Payload, Special Powder, Explosive Chemical Rounds, RJ250, Fat Boy, Cryo Nucleation are some of the most transformative and strong overclocks in the game but thats less than 1/15th of them. A lot tend to just be like +1 magazine worth of ammo or +2 damage which are nice but dont change the game. I do suggest that anyone whos into FPS's and especially co-op horde shooters picks this game up, especially on sale where its only 10 dollars. I think sometimes even down to 7. You really dont have much to lose at that point. So remember to rock and stone or you ain't coming home!
Deep Rock Galactic is one of a few games in my library that I would consider timeless. Especially fun with friends, but a great solo game nonetheless.
This is genuinely the best game I have ever played in my life. If you leave a negative review on this game, you are a pointy eared leaf lover. Rock and Stone!

other than the Gunner and driller having terrible secondaries, game good. 


ROCK AND STONE!!!!!

Phenomenal game. Been playing since launch and it's great. Recently came back to it because it's one of the few games optimized well enough to run on my buddy's laptop. We can't even play tabletop simulator because it overheats his computer and makes it run slow, but DRG is so lightweight at the lower settings that it doesn't even break a sweat. Lots of love for this game and the devs behind it, who communicate fairly frequently about their plans for the game and their commitment to continued development.

Simply my favorite game ever. Easily. The co-op, the voicelines, the atmosphere, the gameplay. It's all very solid and fun to do. On top of it all, a random cave gen system that keeps it fresh after many many hours. Highly recommend!
great game to play with your mates, replay value is fantastic as the map is randomly generated so every experience is different.
killin bugs and beatin off rocks 10/10 game would slam beers with the buddies and mine for gold again

Deep Rock Galactic is simply That Guy. It is the standard of first person co-op shooters that other developers have to meet. It has great variety of classes, weapons, and at the very least 3 completely different ways to play each of the 24 available weapons.

Each weapon has at least 2-3, usually many more ways to build it strong enough for the highest difficulty available. Every class has its separate roles they excel at, and each of the 4 of them is crucial to finishing the mission with the most resources possible and at the fastest time. The game is designed in such a way that players end up coordinating without "stand in a circle" and "ready" checks, the game loop is buttery smooth.

The enemy design is very varied, asking the players to take completely different approaches to different threats. Certain elements are better against certain enemies, physical damage is better against others, and the team is required to have both single target and AoE weapons at all times.

11 missions with hundreds of unique "rooms" the caves generate from, as well as random modifiers to the missions that give higher rewards but increase the difficulty with harder enemies, or draining health, or special events that can spawn during every single run, ensure that the game stays fresh for a very long time.
 
This game is a 9/10. The only thing holding this game back from having a perfect rating is that the meta progression could've been faster, and the perks should be reworked to matter more.

Rock and stone. This game is good, better with friends. The maps are procedurally generated and with several regions to choose from, it take a while to feel stale. The gameplay loop is good, especially when you swap between classes. There are multiple jobs to play through and after years of updates, there are tons of side objectives that can spawn or happen. Because of these random side objectives and procedural map generation, you can binge this game for a few hours and not see the same situation. Eventually the end game becomes grinding out "overclocks" for your characters, which are boosts to your weapons to keep the gameplay loop fresh.

Look, I’ve played a lot of shooters, but nothing quite scratches the itch like Deep Rock Galactic. On paper, it sounds simple: you’re a space dwarf, you go underground, you mine rocks, you shoot giant bugs, and you get out. But the execution? Absolute perfection. It is a masterclass in game design, community building, and pure, unadulterated fun.

Here is exactly why DRG deserves every ounce of praise it gets.

1. The Mining Just Feels Right
In most games, resource gathering is a chore. It’s the boring thing you have to do so you can get back to the "real" game. Not here. The literal act of swinging your pickaxe into a vein of Gold or Nitra is incredibly satisfying. Ghost Ship Games nailed the audio design and tactile feedback. When you hit a chunk of Morkite and it makes that distinct, metallic clink sound, your brain instantly shoots out a hit of dopamine.

Add in the fact that the caves are 100% destructible, and the gameplay loop becomes an absolute blast. Need to get to a high ledge? Dig a staircase. Giant swarm coming? Drill a bunker. You have total freedom to reshape the environment to your advantage, making every single mission feel dynamic.

2. Devs Who Actually Care (And Free Seasonal Events!)
In an era where modern gaming is plagued by predatory microtransactions, battle passes that feel like a second job, and corporate greed, Ghost Ship Games stands out like a beacon of hope. They genuinely love their player base, and it shows in how they handle live service.

The seasonal events aren't cash grabs. They are massive, completely free updates packed with new content, weapons, overclocks, and cosmetic trees. Missed a previous season? No worries—none of the content is gone forever. Everything gets put right into the standard loot pool, meaning there is zero FOMO (Fear Of Missing Out). When the holidays roll around, the Space Rig gets decked out with hilarious decorations, and the devs throw in special event bonus challenges that are just pure fun. They set the gold standard for what game developers should be.

3. The Voice Lines are Instant Classics
You cannot talk about DRG without talking about the voice acting. The dwarves are overflowing with personality, and the voice lines are endlessly quotable. Whether you're spamming the laser pointer at a chunk of gold shouting "WE'RE RICH!" or pointing at a glowing mushroom just to hear "MUSHROOM," the game leans into its own absurdity perfectly.

And, of course, there’s the universal binding agent of the entire community: "ROCK AND STONE!" Pressing a single button to salute your teammates does more for team morale than any voice chat ever could. You accidentally fell off a cliff? Rock and Stone. You just blew up a massive Glyphod Dreadnought? Rock and Stone. You're just standing around doing nothing? Rock and Stone. It creates an instant bond with random players that you just don't find in other gaming communities.

4. Clocking Out: From a Rough Drop to a Beer-Fueled Marathon
The absolute best part of this game, though, is the contrast between the stress of the job and the absolute vibe of the Space Rig afterward.

You could have the most stressful, high-intensity Hazard 5 mission where everything goes wrong. You run out of ammo, Bulk Detonators are tearing through the caves, Cave Leeches are grabbing people from the ceiling, and you barely make it into the Drop Pod with one health point remaining. Your hands are shaking, your eyes are watering, and you're mentally exhausted.

But then, the loading screen finishes. You spawn back into the Space Rig.

The stress instantly evaporates. You walk right over to the Abyss Bar, order a round of Pots 'O' Gold or Oily Oafs from Lloyd, slam them down, and someone immediately runs over to hit the Jukebox. Within thirty seconds, a brutal, exhausting day of mining transforms into a chaotic beer-dancing marathon. You're kicking barrels into the launch bay, spinning around on the dance floor, and throwing mugs at each other until someone drinks a Blackout Stout and collapses on the floor.

The Verdict
Deep Rock Galactic isn't just a great game because the mechanics are solid (which they are). It’s a great game because it has a soul. It’s built on teamwork, excellent developer communication, and a community that is genuinely welcoming to greenbeards and greybeards alike.

If you haven't played it yet, Dont even think about questioning it, grab your pickaxe, load your shotgun, and get down there.

Rock and Stone!

I should've written a review for this one a long time ago, but where do I even start? This game is everything i want in a sci fi horde shooter.
[h3] Gameplay [/h3]
+ Gunplay is fantastic. Lots of variety in the different guns available for each class and they're all really fun to shoot. Furthermore, the upgrade system affords a lot of customization towards how you want to use the gun, which gets amplified even further when you consider overclocks, an unlockable upgrade system that can drastically modify how the gun functions.
+ Plenty of progression available. Weapons have plenty of upgrades to unlock, there's lots and lots of cosmetics to get to make your dwarf as heavy duty or as silly looking as you'd like. It took me probably around 200-300 hours just to unlock all the weapon overclocks, and I still haven't gotten all the cosmetic ones.
+ Each mission feels unique. Multiple different biomes with different hazards, each mission is randomly generated (but in the best way), random modifiers on some missions. Missions feel just familiar enough that you're not lost on what to do, but don't feel like you're just doing the same thing over and over.
+ Each of the 4 classes feels like its got its own role and place on the team. Each class has its own set of weapons, its own utility to help the team, its own way of traversing the map. You can have more than one of a class on the same team, but a lot of players prefer to have one of each in a lobby, because they're all useful to have, no class feels underpowered.
+ "Live service" model is the best I've ever seen. All the DLC is strictly cosmetic (though, i might add, even these are worth the money, the cosmetics are great), getting the base game gives you every bit of gameplay content there is to be had. The season passes are free. You can go back and work through old season passes at any time at no cost. 
+ Plenty of options even if you have to play solo. Single player gives you a helper bot that follows your orders and can do basically anything a dwarf can do. Playing in lobbys with randoms is also a great option, everyone is there to have a good time for the most part and there aren't many toxic players (although every once and a while you might run into a bad apple). I've seen some players trek across a map to rez a new player and guide them to extract.
~ There can be a bit of a learning curve at the beginning, since new players need to learn traversal, mission flow, how the classes work together (and what their class is expected to do), not to mention all the enemy types and side content. It can be a bit overwhelming at first, but the player base is generally really beginner friendly and happy to help.
[h3] World [/h3]
+ The theme of "harsh company that only cares about profits sends you to die in a hole" works really well and is very funny at times. 
+ Environments are really nice looking. Dark caves are punctuated with neon lighting. Biomes all look different. Stumbling upon a huge cave with giant pillars of crystal or spouts of lava is still impressive almost 500 hrs in. 
~ The graphics style may be hit or miss for some, but personally i think it looks great. The psuedo low poly style gives the game a lot of charm.

In all honesty if you've gotten this far, DRG is probably for you. I really don't have much negative things to say about it. If you give it a little time to learn the systems and traversal, you'll be rewarded with a highly replayable and unique sci fi shooter.

Why didn't I play this game sooner is want I think on the daily during my numerous dives to Infuse Core Matrixes.

To anyone looking for an epic, stylish cooperation shooter where everyone needs to play and public servers are welcoming, this is the game.

Rapid summery of why its awesome :
- The progression of this game is like compressed gold
- The devs are involved and their live-service model cannot possibly earn them money
- The gameplay is made for cooperation.
 

The progression is divided in two main parts. The Promotion and the Chase of Overclocks.
You will start with nothing, though you won't be bombarded with thousands of new weapons you should unlock or things that you can't even touch. All you have is access to the 4 classes with only a weapon and grenades.
The more you continue, the more you unlock the basic gear. More grenades, upgrades to your weapons and at level 10, more weapons. Finally, after much hard work, and better understanding the basics of resources and how to earn equipements, you unlock the key to the rest of the game : a promotion.

With it, you unlock the Forge and Deep-Dives two things that will help in the true game : earning Overclocks.
This is when the game starts to hinder your progression, you can't farm matrix cores. You need to wait, and if the mines continue to call, you can start wanting to upgrades the other classes. This cycle will continue until you have everything the game can offer you which can take a lot of hours. This is a good progression, making you want to earn more while not giving you every tools freely. And the first half of progression is an incredible introduction to the main mechanics of the game and doesn't throw everything at you frame one.



Then there is the live service. Like every game released at this period, this game is a live-service though unlike any other (except one which is MCC which aren't new games). Every season, there is an entirely free 100 reward and cosmetic tree. These haven't any gameplay around it, they are completely optional but the developers made them free just because they respect our time and money we spent.
But there is also the additions to the game. Even if nowadays updates won't change much of the core gameplay of the game, there are still new events and biome added, with enemies and hazards you need to worry about at any missions. The devs continue to update the game and have listened to the community numerous times throughout the 6 years of developpement.
Finally and I am shocked that they did this incredible thing : Every event from previous seasons can still be experienced to this day with the cosmetics still unlockable.
No joke, this is the most consumer friendly live service game on the market. I cannot see a better alternative.



Finally, the 4 classes with a maximum of 4 dwarves per missions (without mods) is obviously screaming for diversity of playstyle within a group.
And the gameplay clearly incentivize this repartition. The classes all have unique traits that makes them needed throughout a mission :
The Driller can drill and their weapons are crowd control machines. Able to reshape the terrain to their advantage, creating alternative routes and displacing objectives. A creative player might also found more uses to take the advantage over the glyphids.
The Scout is your dexterous god, able to go mine every last ores on the cavern ceiling while still able to dish out massive damage on one enemy. Their flare guns makes the caves unable to hide its secrets. Though more of a specialist, they are incredibly useful in a team. Especially paired with
The Engineer. If there is a swiss army knife, it would be them. Multiple angles to cover ? Turrets. A large horde ? Distraction and Semi-auto shotgun. Needing a bridge ? Platforms. A strong bug just spawned ? Grenade Launcher. Though good at seemingly everything, they will need the most ammo of the group and with a big enough swarm, they can be overwelmed.
And if the Glyphid swarm finally arrived and all hope is lost to our 3 dwarves, the Gunner enter in a glorious rescue. They have the firepower and the utility all of the others lack. To destroy swarms, you need the guns, to revive in a crowd of bugs, you need a shield and to transport easily over long distances, you need a zipline. All of these are covered with the Gunner.

Though this perfectly balance class system wouldn't be useful if the enemies weren't good at their job.

I wrote a bit more that I would've liked so I won't larp on it as much. To put it simply, a great deal of bugs are made with teamwork in mind. Stationary creatures which destroyable bullets, weakpoints on the rear of bugs and cave leaches that needs to be killed by a teammate before it releases its prey. The objectives are also all thought with the classes in mind. Some may be easier with the Driller, the Engineer, the Scout or the Gunner. It all depends on the mission and its hazards.



Well I talked enough about this game. Its awesome and anyone that might have the tiniest bit of hesitation should try it. Most people won't regret it.

To finalize, did you know that this game handles mods ? Especially gameplay altering mods which is INSANE. A live service able to entertain the idea of modding. I didn't expect that when I bought it, but I was pleasantly surprised.
the company's lack of care for the need of better equipment kills me more than the bugs but thats why its really fun

I truly love this game. A friend had gifted it to me so we could play together when Helldivers 2 was in a really bad spot (when isn't it) and it didn't really click with me at first. And then, suddenly it did. I genuinely couldn't stop coming back for more. There's just something so genius about the mix of traditional fantasy with sci-fi that makes for such a rich world full of charm and personality. 
Dwarves mining for resources on an alien planet? What a wonderful idea! The classes all feel good and have a good balance of strengths and weaknesses. All DLC is cosmetic and the passes are always available to you. Soundtrack is great for the atmosphere when exploring and the intensity of swarms and bosses. Customization is great with countless combinations. There's a number of recreational activities on the space rig. BEER AND SEXY DWARVES. What's there not to like? Absolutely strong recommendation. Rock and Stone!

Great game to play with friends. Varied playstyles, fun enemies and challenges, unique environments, quirky characters. All the little easter eggs make this game entertaining, even when when you're just waiting in the pre-game lobby. Things can get somewhat repetitive after a while, but you could squeeze a good 50-100 hours out of it.

[h1]The Ultimate Science Fiction Dwarf Fantasy![/h1]

This game is a true marvel, and one I didn't expect. Deep Rock Galactic delicately balances whimsy and seriousness, and it strikes that balance just right: The challenges you face on missions feel deeply real and suck you into the game. That tension is frequently broken up by whimsical mechanics like sliding on pipelines through self-drilled tunnels or just by enjoying a drink, or dance, or both (at the same time) in the lobby. The graphics, sound design and especially the music underline the atmosphere. It grabs on to your inner dwarf. It refines that energy, that feeling, and launches it into deep space! Deep Rock Galactic made me feel the most dwarven I have ever felt playing any video game, ever.

[h1]Mechanics[/h1]
Combat mechanics provide a challenge without being overly punishing for new or casual players. The monsters each have different strengths, weaknesses and attack patterns. They are interesting to learn and introduced gradually so that your skill can keep up with the difficulty. The four classes complement each other well, even when you have only two players in a session. 

[h1]Price[/h1]
I got the game for 8,99€ in a sale, and it's a steal at that price. The non-discounted price of 29,99€ is a bit steep as with most games, but not unreasonably expensive. If you have friends to play with it could be worth it. I think I would personally be happy to pay 20€-25€ based on my current experience with the game.

Honestly, just buy it.
If you’re tired of toxic competitive shooters and games that feel like a second job, Deep Rock Galactic is exactly the palate cleanser you need. It’s basically Left 4 Dead meets Minecraft, but with space Dwarves, and it absolutely rules.

I've put a ton of hours into this, and it’s easily one of the best co-op experiences out there. Here is the actual breakdown of why it's so good:

The Gameplay Loop
You and up to three others drop into 100% destructible alien caves to mine minerals and fight off massive hordes of bugs. There are four classes—Gunner, Scout, Engineer, and Driller—and none of them feel like an afterthought. The synergy is perfect. An Engineer can shoot a platform onto a wall, the Scout grapples up to mine the gold, and the Driller burns a tunnel straight to the extraction pod when things go south. Every class feels powerful and necessary.

The Best Community in Gaming
Online gaming can be a dumpster fire, but the DRG community is shockingly wholesome. A huge part of this is the dedicated salute button. You hit 'V', your Dwarf screams "Rock and Stone!", and instantly everyone is on the same page. You can queue up with total strangers without mics and still have a flawless, coordinated run because everyone just wants to be a good teammate.

Zero FOMO ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥
The developers (Ghost Ship Games) actually respect their players.

The battle passes are completely free.

If you miss a season, the cosmetics don't disappear forever—they just get moved into the regular in-game loot pools for you to find later.

There’s no aggressive monetization pushing you to spend extra cash just to look cool.

Bottom Line
Whether you're sweating through a high-stakes Hazard 5 dive or just screwing around kicking barrels into the launch bay on the Space Rig, this game is pure fun. If you have a group of friends to play with—or even if you don't—it's worth every penny.

Rock and Stone!

IMO, the best co-op game ever made.  Deep gameplay, an amazing community, and equally amazing devs.  There's a reason people in comment sections all over the internet get fanatical when this game is mentioned.
Sometimes, I'll even spend hours just goofing around in the hub for no other reason than because it's fun.

Now, if you'll excuse me, I have to go drink copious amounts of beer during work hours while handling dangerous weapons.  Rock and stone, everyone.
